cases.description: Basic numeric text conversion with explicit US-style decimal_separator and group_separator arguments
European-style formatted number: ',' is passed as decimal_separator and '.' as group_separator, letting NUMBERVALUE parse locale-formatted text regardless of the engine's own locale settings
cases.expected_note: Matches Microsoft's own documented example. Source: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/numbervalue-function-1b05c8cf-2bfa-4437-af70-596c7ea7d879

cases.description: Text that cannot be parsed as a number at all -> #VALUE!
cases.expected_note: Source: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/numbervalue-function-1b05c8cf-2bfa-4437-af70-596c7ea7d879 - 'If any of the arguments are not valid, NUMBERVALUE returns the #VALUE! error value'

cases.description: A decimal separator appearing more than once in the text is documented as invalid input -> #VALUE!
cases.expected_note: Source: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/numbervalue-function-1b05c8cf-2bfa-4437-af70-596c7ea7d879 - decimal separator used multiple times is explicitly called out as an error condition
